name: Egyptian Chocolate Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour, baking powder, cinnamon, and cloves. Set aside.
2. In a small saucepan, combine chocolate and coffee. Cook over low heat until the chocolate is melted, stirring constantly. Remove from heat and cool to room temperature.
3. Cream the butter and sugar in a mixing bowl until they are light and fluffy.
4. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Beat in vanilla extract.
5. Gradually add the chocolate mixture and dry ingredients alternately with milk to the creamed mixture, beating well after each addition.
6. Pour batter into 2 greased and waxed paper-lined 8-inch cake pans.
7. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for 30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Cool in pans on racks for 10 minutes. Remove from pans; cool completely on racks.
8. To assemble the cake, place one cake layer on a serving plate. Spread with Cinnamon Whipped Cream. Top with second cake layer.
9. Frost sides and top with remaining Cinnamon Whipped Cream. Refrigerate until serving time.
Cinnamon Whipped Cream:
1. Chill large mixing bowl and beaters.
2. Combine cream, sugar, vanilla extract, and cinnamon in a mixing bowl. Beat with an electric mixer set at high speed until soft peaks form and the mixture is thick enough to spread. Do not overbeat; otherwise, it will become butter instead of whipped cream.
